 {"id":"bd-0bu2","title":"Implement IrGanttMeta for gantt chart date-aware features","description":"# Gantt Chart IR Metadata

## Purpose
Gantt charts need date-aware metadata that the generic IR doesn't support. This task adds gantt-specific metadata structures.

## Types to Add
1. IrGanttMeta { date_format: String, title: Option<String>, excludes: Vec<GanttExclude>, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> }
2. IrGanttSection { name: String, tasks: Vec<IrGanttTask> }
3. IrGanttTask { id: String, name: String, task_type: GanttTaskType, start: GanttDate, end: GanttDate, depends_on: Vec<String>, progress: Option<f32> }
4. GanttTaskType enum: Normal, Critical, Done, Active, Milestone
5. GanttDate enum: Absolute(String), AfterTask(String), Duration(String)
6. GanttExclude enum: Weekends, Dates(Vec<String>)
7. Add gantt_meta: Option<IrGanttMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

## This unblocks: gantt date parsing and gantt SVG rendering tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:41.257934926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:55:17.607818688Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:55:17.607344110Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","gantt","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-0bu2","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":197,"issue_id":"bd-0bu2","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL FIELDS NEEDED in IrGanttMeta:
- tick_interval: Option<GanttTickInterval> (Day, Week, Month, Quarter, Year)
- axis_format: Option<String> (strftime format for axis labels)  
- inclusive_end_dates: bool
- today_marker_style: Option<String> (CSS-like style string)
- weekday_start: Option<u8> (0=Sunday through 6=Saturday)

These are required for full gantt parity.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-0hz0","title":"Bug hunt: SVG dimension parsing should use root tag","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:22:58.694428007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:24:57.575563212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T04:24:57.575283813Z","close_reason":"Extract SVG dimensions from root <svg> tag","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0n3y","title":"Sequence: support autonumber start and increment parity","description":"# Sequence autonumber parameter parity

Mermaid sequence diagrams support more than bare `autonumber`; they also support explicit numeric parameters such as `autonumber 10` and `autonumber 10 5`.

Scope
- Parse `autonumber <start>` and `autonumber <start> <increment>` in sequence diagrams
- Store the starting number and increment in sequence IR metadata
- Render numbered sequence message labels using the configured progression instead of always starting at 1 with step 1
- Preserve existing bare `autonumber` behavior as start=1 increment=1
- Add parser/core/render regression coverage

Acceptance Criteria
- No unsupported-syntax warning for parameterized `autonumber` lines
- IR round-trips the configured numbering parameters cleanly
- SVG numbering reflects configured start and increment deterministically
- Focused tests plus relevant workspace quality gates pass via `rch` for cargo operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:16.938161393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108872718Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108376919Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence autonumber parameter parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0pi0","title":"Packet-Beta: Implement bit numbering and field type rendering","description":"# Packet-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Bit numbering along top/bottom of packet header not rendered
- Field type/description text not rendered within fields
- Byte alignment grid not rendered
- Field styling by type

## Implementation Plan
1. Render bit numbering as small labels along the top edge of the packet
2. Show field name and optional type centered within each field box
3. Draw byte alignment markers at 8-bit boundaries
4. Apply distinct fill colors for different field types if categorized","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.697546308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:15:19.097026025Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:15:19.096741096Z","close_reason":"Replaced generic flowchart-style packet parser with proper bit-range field parsing (0-7: "Label" syntax). Fields get packet-field CSS class, packet-bits-N width class, packet-field-wide for >8 bit fields. Fields linked sequentially. Label shows field name with bit range. Fallback to generic parsing for non-range syntax. 3 new tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["packet-beta","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-0pi0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:43Z"},{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-0pi0","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Packet with 3 fields spanning 0-7, 8-15, 16-31 → verify bit number labels along top
2. Field with type annotation → verify type text centered in field box
3. 32-bit packet → verify byte alignment markers at bits 8, 16, 24
4. Field spanning entire row → verify full-width rendering
5. Narrow 4-bit field → verify field is readable even when small","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-0wdt","title":"Audit and update README Limitations section for accuracy","description":"## Problem
The README Limitations section is a good-faith disclosure but may be stale or incomplete after recent work. It currently lists:
1. XyChart unsupported (parses but lacks dedicated layout/rendering)
2. Sequence advanced features not implemented
3. classDef/style directives not applied
4. Very large SVGs heavy for browsers
5. PNG export doesn't preserve animations
6. WebGPU planned but not available
7. Some niche Mermaid syntax may differ

## Scope
After other beads in this epic are closed, update the Limitations section to:
1. Remove items that are now implemented (e.g., if classDef gets wired).
2. Add any NEW limitations discovered (e.g., xyChart fidelity, long-tail diagram visual quality).
3. Add the FNX disclosure (cross-reference with bd-7xyz).
4. Ensure each limitation has actionable guidance (workaround or expected timeline).
5. Verify the "sequence diagram" description matches actual state after bd-t0hz.

## Dependencies
This should be done LAST in the epic, after all other fixes land.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Limitations section accurately reflects current state.
- No limitation listed that has been resolved.
- No known limitation omitted.
- Each limitation has a workaround or "tracked for implementation" note.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:32.325627191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.071694082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.071160051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-65l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:45.721050217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-7xyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:45.994214527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-jmgo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:45.585645586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-k3bv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:45.857157889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-t0hz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:46.130614773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-uwtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:46.266431544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0wdt","depends_on_id":"bd-w9id","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:45.452626942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
